goal: Generate a compact landscape academic companion figure explaining how tracked objects become zone-based observation variables.

style.visual_language: clean explanatory panel with crisp blocks and thin arrows

article_context.paper_theme: observation-level framework for digital twins in manufacturing
article_context.input: tracked people and movable production objects
article_context.zone_rule: zone membership combined with track continuity

instructions: Compose the panel left-to-right in three logical parts.
On the left show a simplified production frame with polygon zones.
In the middle show track fragments and continuity cues.
On the right show derived observation variables as a concise list or small cards.
Keep the text minimal and the shapes crisp.
Make the figure look like a publication figure, not a slide.

panels.label: Zone Membership
panels.description: simplified production scene with polygon zones and object positions

panels.label: Track Continuity
panels.description: track fragments and continuity arrows across time

panels.label: Observation Variables
panels.description: occupancy, entries, exits, dwell time, trajectory density

caption_hint: Zone semantics panel showing how zone membership and track continuity are transformed into observation variables for manufacturing digital twin monitoring.
